profiling_summary: Hotspots: OpenMP barrier 22%, parallel region shows load imbalance, threads spend significant time waiting at synchronization points.

ranked_candidates.target: OpenMP thread placement/affinity to reduce barrier wait and variance
ranked_candidates.rationale: Profiling shows high OpenMP barrier time (22%) and imbalance (1.8); the retrieved pattern recommends CPU affinity (thread pinning) to avoid OS thread migration and reduce synchronization/overhead variability, which can amplify barrier waiting in OpenMP regions.
ranked_candidates.action_sketch: Enable and pin OpenMP threads to cores/sockets using runtime settings (e.g., OMP_PROC_BIND/OMP_PLACES) and keep placement consistent across runs; optionally set close/spread binding to match core topology and reduce cross-core migration overhead during the imbalanced parallel-for.
preconditions: OpenMP runtime supports OMP_PROC_BIND and OMP_PLACES
Job launcher/OS allows setting affinity (no conflicting pinning policies)
Run uses a fixed OMP_NUM_THREADS for comparability
correctness_checks: Verify CHECKSUM output matches baseline for fixed n,skew across affinity settings
Confirm no changes to reduction semantics or floating-point flags that would alter results beyond expected reproducibility
rollback_criteria: Wall time does not improve or regresses by >5% versus baseline
omp_barrier_pct does not decrease or increases materially
Observed CPU migrations/context switches increase under chosen binding
